extraction.one_line_summary: LAPORTE Christophe won via Jumbo-Visma team time trial after stringing out the field on the final climb with 8km to go, gaining 22 seconds on main GC contenders.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma used Teunissen/Hofstede/Laporte rotation on final 8km climb to split field, creating impromptu team time trial finish at 60kph.
Defending champion Schachmann lost 22 seconds unexpectedly on final climb, revealing vulnerability on even moderate gradients when pace is high.
INEOS positioned aggressively for crosswinds despite no GC leader present, suggesting team protocol prioritizes wind splits regardless of leadership status.
Quick-Step chased breakaway for Jakobsen despite final climb profile unsuitable for pure sprinter, tactical miscalculation or desperation move.

rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: negative: dropped on final 8km climb before GC riders, concerning form signal for defending champion
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: negative: lost 22 seconds as defending champion, came in third group rather than second
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: matched team's devastating climb pace despite nearly winning race previous year, form clearly present
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: positive: drove final climb split then outsprinted teammates, indicates exceptional early-season condition for new signing
rider_signals.HOFSTEDE Lennard: positive: performed extended pull at 60kph on final climb, domestique showing unexpected power reserve
